channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Opus 4.7
item.description: May 22, 10:31 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. May 22, 10:26 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. May 22, 10:17 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Zhipu AI Launches GLM-5.1 High-Speed API: 400 Tokens/s Sets New Global Benchmark
item.description: Zhipu AI has launched GLM-5.1-highspeed, an API variant of its GLM-5.1 model delivering 400 tokens per second — reportedly the fastest inference speed among major global LLM providers.

item.title: Google I/O showed how the path for AI-driven science is shifting
item.description: During Tuesday’s Google I/O keynote, Demis Hassabis, the CEO of Google DeepMind, proclaimed that we are currently “standing in the foothills of the singularity.” It was a striking statement—the singularity is the theoretical future moment when AI rapidly exceeds human intelligence and dramatically...

item.title: HiDream AI Unveils Unified Multimodal Model with 200B+ Parameters, Raises New Funding
item.description: HiDream AI has unveiled HiDream-O1-Image-Pro, a native unified multimodal model with over 200 billion parameters, alongside announcing a new billion-level funding round.

item.title: ByteDance Releases Lance, a Lightweight Native Unified Multimodal AI Model
item.description: ByteDance has released Lance, an open-source native unified multimodal AI model with only 3 billion activated parameters, designed to handle image understanding, generation, and video tasks in a single system.

item.title: CapCut Partners with Google Gemini to Bring AI Video Editing Directly to the Gemini App
item.description: ByteDance's CapCut has announced a partnership with Google Gemini, enabling users to access CapCut's video editing tools directly within the Gemini app for AI-assisted content creation.

item.title: Anker Soundcore Liberty 5 Pro Launches with Self-Developed AI Audio Chip, Sets Guinness Record
item.description: Anker has unveiled the Soundcore Liberty 5 Pro and Pro Max wireless earbuds at its first-ever product launch event, featuring a proprietary AI audio chip that has earned a Guinness World Record.

item.title: ‘First VPN’ Cybercrime Service Disrupted, Administrator Arrested
item.description: The FBI says First VPN has been used by dozens of ransomware groups for network reconnaissance and intrusions. The post ‘First VPN’ Cybercrime Service Disrupted, Administrator Arrested appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: The Enhanced Games fit right in with the rest of 2026’s longevity vibes
item.description: This Sunday, a group of 42 athletes will gather in Las Vegas to compete in a somewhat unusual sporting competition. Participants in the inaugural Enhanced Games are being encouraged to take performance-enhancing drugs. The goal is to “push the boundaries of human performance.” The games’ organizers...

item.title: Kimwolf DDoS Botnet Operator Arrested in Canada Over DDoS-for-Hire Attacks
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice (DoJ) on Thursday announced the arrest of a Canadian man in connection with allegedly operating a distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) botnet known as Kimwolf. In tandem, Jacob Butler (aka Dort), 23, Ottawa, Canada, has been charged with offenses related to the...

item.title: TrendAI Patches Apex One Zero-Day Exploited in the Wild
item.description: CVE-2026-34926 is a directory traversal flaw that can be exploited against the on-premise version of Apex One. The post TrendAI Patches Apex One Zero-Day Exploited in the Wild appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Cloudflare One DNS issues with .internal zones
item.description: May 22, 08:06 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. May 22, 08:02 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. May 22, 07:52 UTC Identified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. May 22, 07:52 UTC Investigating - We are...

item.title: Grafana Says Codebase and Other Data Stolen via TanStack Supply Chain Attack
item.description: Hackers accessed Grafana’s GitHub repositories after a token compromised in the TanStack attack was not rotated. The post Grafana Says Codebase and Other Data Stolen via TanStack Supply Chain Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Elevated error rate on multiple models
item.description: May 22, 06:32 UTCUpdate - We are monitoring recovery on Claude Opus 4.7, and working to fully resolve remaining errors impacting Haiku 4.5. We will provide additional updates shortly.May 22, 06:17 UTCUpdate - We are currently working to resolve some additional errors impacting Claude Opus 4.7 and...

item.title: [AINews] New AI Infra unicorns: Exa, Modal, TurboPuffer
item.description: a quiet day lets us feature fundraises!

item.title: CISA Adds Exploited Langflow and Trend Micro Apex One Vulnerabilities to KEV
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Thursday added two security flaws impacting Langflow and Trend Micro Apex One to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, citing evidence of active exploitation. The vulnerabilities in question are listed below -...

item.title: Cisco Patches CVSS 10.0 Secure Workload REST API Flaw Enabling Data Access
item.description: Cisco has rolled out updates for a maximum-severity security flaw impacting Secure Workload that could allow an unauthenticated, remote attacker to access sensitive data. Tracked as CVE-2026-20223 (CVSS score: 10.0), the vulnerability arises from insufficient validation and authentication when...

item.title: Increased HTTP 5xx Errors to us-east-1 from Western North America and Asia Pacific co-locations.
item.description: May 22, 04:08 UTCResolved - Cloudflare saw an increase in 5xx errors to us-east-1 from Western North America and Asia Pacific co-locations from 04:08 AM UTC to 04:35 AM UTC. Issue has now been resolved

item.title: $ECUAS_n$: A family of metrics for principled evaluation of uncertainty-augmented systems
item.description: arXiv:2605.20490v2 Announce Type: new Abstract: In high-stakes automated decision-making, access to predictive uncertainty is essential for enabling users -- human or downstream systems -- to accept or reject predictions based on application-specific cost trade-offs. Such uncertainty-augmented (UA)...

item.title: Open-World Evaluations for Measuring Frontier AI Capabilities
item.description: arXiv:2605.20520v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Benchmark-based evaluation remains important for tracking frontier AI progress. But it can both overstate and understate deployed capability because it privileges tasks that can be precisely specified, automatically graded, easy to optimize for, and...

item.title: AgentAtlas: Beyond Outcome Leaderboards for LLM Agents
item.description: arXiv:2605.20530v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language model agents now act on codebases, browsers, operating systems, calendars, files, and tool ecosystems, but the benchmarks used to evaluate them are fragmented: each emphasizes a different unit of measurement (final task success,...

item.title: Personality Engineering with AI Agents: A New Methodology for Negotiation Research
item.description: arXiv:2605.20554v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: According to canonical negotiation theory, people's success in a negotiation depends on how well they balance competing demands--empathizing and asserting, demonstrating concern for other and concern for self, being soft on the people and hard on the...

item.title: Mahjax: A GPU-Accelerated Mahjong Simulator for Reinforcement Learning in JAX
item.description: arXiv:2605.20577v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Riichi Mahjong is a multi-player, imperfect-information game characterized by stochasticity and high-dimensional state spaces. These attributes present a unique combination of challenges that mirror complex real-world decision-making problems in...

item.title: Automated Kernel Discovery Towards Understanding High-dimensional Bayesian Optimization
item.description: arXiv:2605.20249v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Gaussian Process (GP) kernels are central to Bayesian optimization (BO), yet designing effective kernels for high-dimensional problems still relies on extensive manual engineering. Existing automated approaches struggle in high dimensions for two...

item.title: Physics-informed convolutional neural networks for fluid flow through porous media
item.description: arXiv:2605.20250v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Accurate simulation of fluid flow in porous media is challenging due to complex pore-space geometries and the computational cost of solving the Navier-Stokes equations. This difficulty is particularly important when repeated simulations are required,...

item.title: Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning for Safe Autonomous Driving Under Pedestrian Behavioral Uncertainty
item.description: arXiv:2605.20255v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Simulation-based testing of self-driving cars (SDCs) typically relies on scripted or simplified pedestrian models that do not capture the heterogeneity and uncertainty of real human crossing behavior. This limits the realism of safety assessments,...

item.title: FBOS-RL: Feedback-Driven Bi-Objective Synergistic Reinforcement Learning
item.description: arXiv:2605.20256v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reinforcement learning has become a cornerstone for aligning and unlocking the reasoning capabilities of large-scale models. At its core, the training loop of GRPO and its variants alternates between rollout sampling and policy update. Unlike...

item.title: Instance Discrimination for Link Prediction
item.description: arXiv:2605.20257v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recently, instance discrimination models have emerged as a major solution for self-supervised learning. Having already demonstrated its effectiveness in the image domain, instance discrimination learning is now proving equally convincing in the graph...

item.title: CR4T: Rewrite-Based Guardrails for Adolescent LLM Safety
item.description: arXiv:2605.21609v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly embedded in adolescent digital environments, mediating information seeking, advice, and emotionally sensitive interactions. Yet existing safety mechanisms remain largely grounded in adult-centric norms and...

item.title: Broadening Access to Transportation Safety Data with Generative AI: A Schema-Grounded Framework for Spatial Natural Language Queries
item.description: arXiv:2605.21712v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Transportation safety analysis requires integrating crash records, roadway attributes, and geospatial data through GIS-based workflows, but access remains uneven across agencies and community stakeholders. Technical prerequisites create a gap between...

item.title: Sem-Detect: Semantic Level Detection of AI Generated Peer-Reviews
item.description: arXiv:2605.21713v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: How can we distinguish whether a peer review was written by a human or generated by an AI model? We argue that, in this setting, authorship should not be attributed solely from the textual features of a review, but also from the ideas, judgments, and...

item.title: Probabilistic Attribution For Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2605.21726v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The generative nature of Large Language Models (LLMs) is reflected in the conditional probabilities they compute to sample each response token given the previous tokens. These probabilities encode the distributional structure that the model learns in...

item.title: RankJudge: A Multi-Turn LLM-as-a-Judge Synthetic Benchmark Generator
item.description: arXiv:2605.21748v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As interactive LLM-based applications are created and refined, model developers need to evaluate the quality of generated text along many possible axes. For simpler systems, human evaluation may be practical, but in complicated systems like...

item.title: Elevated latency and error rates for ChatGPT 5.5 Thinking
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Conversations (Operational)

item.title: Alleged Kimwolf Botmaster ‘Dort’ Arrested, Charged in U.S. and Canada
item.description: Canadian authorities on Wednesday arrested a 23-year-old Ottawa man on suspicion of building and operating Kimwolf, a fast spreading Internet-of-Things botnet that enslaved millions of devices for use in a series of massive distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks over the past six months....

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude.ai
item.description: May 21, 21:08 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.May 21, 20:42 UTCMonitoring - We have applied a fix and success rates for requests on Claude.ai have returned to normal. We are monitoring closely to ensure no further issues.May 21, 20:24 UTCInvestigating - We are investigating elevated...

item.title: Roundtables: Can AI Learn to Understand the World?
item.description: Listen to the session or watch below AI companies want to build systems that understand the external world and overcome the limitations of LLMs. Recent developments have brought world models to the forefront of the AI discussion. Watch a conversation with editor in chief Mat Honan, senior AI editor...

item.title: Giving Agents Computers — Ivan Burazin, Daytona
item.description: We chat with Daytona's CEO about their insane 74% MoM Growth, 850K Daily Runs, Bare Metal Sandboxes, RL Evals, and the New Agent Cloud

item.title: We’re launching the Google DeepMind Accelerator program in Asia Pacific to tackle environmental risks
Scaling creativity in the age of AI
item.description: Storytelling is core to humanity’s DNA, stemming from our impulse to express ideals, warnings, hopes, and experiences. Technology has always been woven through the medium and the distribution: from early humans’ innovation of natural pigments and charcoals for cave paintings to literal...

item.title: Elevated error rates on ChatGPT paid plans
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Conversations (Operational)

item.title: Cloudflare Containers Issues
item.description: May 21, 14:38 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is investigating issues with starting new containers. We are working to understand the full impact and mitigate this problem. More updates to follow shortly.

item.title: Anthropic’s Code with Claude showed off coding’s future—whether you like it or not
item.description: The vibes were strong at Code with Claude, Anthropic’s two-day event for software developers in London that kicked off on May 19, the same day as Google’s I/O in Palo Alto. (A coincidence, not a flex, Anthropic staffers assured me.) “Who here has shipped a pull request in the last week that was...

item.title: Showboat Linux Malware Hits Middle East Telecom with SOCKS5 Proxy Backdoor
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new Linux malware dubbed Showboat that has been put to use in a campaign targeting a telecommunications provider in the Middle East since at least mid-2022. "Showboat is a modular post-exploitation framework designed for Linux systems, capable...

item.title: Cisco Patches Critical Vulnerability in Secure Workload
item.description: Insufficient validation and authentication in the Secure Workload’s REST APIs provide remote attackers with Site Admin privileges. The post Cisco Patches Critical Vulnerability in Secure Workload appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Linux Rootkits, Router 0-Day, AI Intrusions, Scam Kits and 25 New Stories
item.description: This week starts small. A token leaks. A bad package slips in. A login trick works. An old tool shows up again. At first, it feels like the usual mess. Then you see the pattern: attackers are not always breaking in. They are using the parts we already trust. That is what makes it worrying. The...

item.title: Ocean Emerges From Stealth With $28M for Agentic Email Security Platform
item.description: The company has developed a platform that uses specialized AI agents to inspect every incoming message. The post Ocean Emerges From Stealth With $28M for Agentic Email Security Platform appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: [AINews] OpenAI GPT-next disproves 80 year old Erdős planar unit distance problem for under $1000
item.description: a quiet day but a nice result in AI x mathematics

item.title: Increased 5XX errors in Porto Alegre and Curitiba, Brazil
item.description: May 21, 07:27 UTCResolved - Cloudflare saw an Increased 5XX errors in Porto Alegre and Curitiba, Brazil from 06:28 AM - 07:02 AM UTC. Issue has now been resolved

item.title: Railway: The Agent-Native Cloud — Jake Cooper
item.description: 3M Users, 100K Signups/Week, Own-Metal Data Centers, $200K+ Coding Agent Spend, and the Death of PRs
